description: Runtime local-first para Claude Code: memória persistente, rotinas autônomas e bootstrap inteligente via scanner de transcripts. Instalável via `continuum init`.

userStories.title: Project scaffolding and pyproject.toml
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the project structure so that I can install continuum as a Python package with CLI entry point
acceptanceCriteria: pyproject.toml with [project] metadata, version 0.1.0, python>=3.10
Entry point: continuum = 'continuum.cli:main'
src/continuum/ package with __init__.py (version string), cli.py (click-based CLI skeleton with init/scan/run/status/doctor subcommands that print 'not implemented yet')
README.md with project description, installation, quick start
LICENSE file (MIT)
.gitignore for Python
`pip install -e .` works and `continuum --help` shows all commands
Typecheck passes or no type errors
userStories.notes: Use click for CLI. Minimal deps: click, toml (stdlib in 3.11+), pathlib. No Flask, no heavy deps.

userStories.title: Config system and continuum.toml
userStories.description: As a user, I want a configuration file so that I can customize my continuum installation
acceptanceCriteria: src/continuum/config.py with Config dataclass/model
Reads from .continuum/config/continuum.toml
Default config generated on init with sensible defaults
Config fields: project.name, project.domain, project.language, scanner.enabled, scanner.sanitization (strict/balanced/raw), skills.prefix, scheduler.enabled
Config loads with defaults for missing fields
Config accessible via `from continuum.config import load_config`
userStories.notes: Use tomllib (stdlib 3.11+) for reading, tomli-w or manual string for writing. Keep it simple.

userStories.title: continuum init - interactive bootstrap
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run `continuum init` to set up the system interactively
acceptanceCriteria: Prompts for: project name (default: directory name), work domain, language (default: en), skill prefix (default: cx)
Creates directory structure: .continuum/{config,memory/{bootstrap,working,consolidated},skills/{core,local},reports,runtime/{runs,logs,state},templates}
Generates continuum.toml from answers
Generates CLAUDE.md template in .continuum/templates/ referencing memory paths and conventions
Generates empty memory files: .continuum/memory/working/debugging.md, preferences.md, profile.md
Prints summary of what was created
If .continuum/ already exists, warns and asks to overwrite or abort
Works when run from any directory
userStories.notes: Use click.prompt for interactive input. CLAUDE.md template should be a useful starting point that tells Claude where memory lives, what conventions to follow.

userStories.title: Transcript discovery and parser
userStories.description: As a user, I want continuum to find and parse my Claude Code session transcripts
acceptanceCriteria: src/continuum/scanner/discover.py finds all .jsonl files under ~/.claude/projects/*/
Handles nested project paths (e.g. -home-user-projects-foo)
src/continuum/scanner/parser.py parses each .jsonl line into structured messages
Extracts: role, text content, tool calls (name + args summary), files mentioned, commands mentioned
Returns list of Session objects with project_path, messages, timestamp estimates
Handles malformed lines gracefully (skip with warning)
Reports stats: N sessions found, M messages total, top projects by volume
userStories.notes: JSONL format is one JSON object per line. Each line has 'type' field. Look for 'human' and 'assistant' message types. Tool use is nested in content blocks.

userStories.title: Heuristic extractor - preferences and corrections
userStories.description: As a user, I want the scanner to extract my preferences and corrections from transcripts without using an LLM
acceptanceCriteria: src/continuum/scanner/heuristics.py with extract_preferences() and extract_corrections()
Detects explicit preferences via pattern matching: language preference, verbosity, style directives (be concise, no fluff, step by step, etc)
Detects corrections: messages following assistant output that contain negation patterns (não, no not that, instead do, don't, stop doing, wrong)
Detects technology preferences: programming languages, frameworks, tools mentioned frequently
Detects project structure patterns: common directory names, config files, build tools
Returns structured dict with confidence scores (based on frequency)
Works on the Session objects from US-004
userStories.notes: Pure Python, no ML. Regex + frequency counting. Support both English and Portuguese patterns. This is the deterministic foundation before any LLM pass.

userStories.title: Sanitizer and bootstrap memory writer
userStories.description: As a user, I want extracted data to be sanitized before being written as bootstrap memory
acceptanceCriteria: src/continuum/scanner/sanitize.py with sanitize() function
Three modes: strict (remove all named entities, paths, specific values), balanced (keep tech terms and patterns, remove personal data), raw (keep everything)
Removes apparent secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords matching common patterns)
src/continuum/scanner/bootstrap.py writes sanitized data to .continuum/memory/bootstrap/
Generates: user_profile.md (human-readable summary), preferences.json, corrections.json, tech_stack.json, topics.json
bootstrap_summary.md with overview of what was extracted and confidence levels
userStories.notes: Sanitization is critical for trust. When in doubt, remove. The balanced mode should be safe for most users.

userStories.title: continuum scan - full pipeline
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run `continuum scan` to analyze my transcripts and create bootstrap memory
acceptanceCriteria: `continuum scan` runs the full pipeline: discover -> parse -> heuristics -> sanitize -> write
Shows progress: 'Found N sessions...', 'Analyzing...', 'Extracting preferences...', 'Writing bootstrap memory...'
Accepts --sanitization flag (strict/balanced/raw, default: balanced)
Accepts --max-sessions flag (default: 200) to limit processing
Accepts --dry-run flag to show what would be extracted without writing
Prints summary: preferences found, corrections found, tech stack, top topics
Handles case where no transcripts exist (prints helpful message)
Writes results to .continuum/memory/bootstrap/
userStories.notes: This is the killer feature. Make the output clear and trustworthy. User should feel confident in what was extracted.

userStories.title: Skill manifest and runner
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run skills that read memory and produce outputs
acceptanceCriteria: src/continuum/skills/manifest.py defines Skill dataclass from skill.yaml
Skill fields: id, name, description, version, triggers (schedule/manual), inputs, outputs, capabilities, entrypoint
src/continuum/skills/runner.py loads skill manifest, resolves inputs, executes entrypoint, captures output
Skills can be: a prompt.md file (run via claude -p), or a Python script
Runner logs execution to .continuum/runtime/runs/<timestamp>_<skill-id>/
Each run has: manifest.json (copy), output.txt, status.json (started_at, completed_at, exit_code)
`continuum run <skill-id>` finds skill by id in .continuum/skills/ and runs it
`continuum skills list` shows available skills with status
userStories.notes: For prompt-based skills, shell out to `claude -p <prompt> --max-turns 15`. For Python skills, subprocess. Keep it simple.

userStories.title: Built-in skills: consolidate, reflect, discover
userStories.description: As a user, I want built-in skills that work out of the box
acceptanceCriteria: 3 built-in skills installed to .continuum/skills/core/ during init
consolidate-state: reads .continuum/memory/working/*.md, summarizes recent changes, writes to consolidated/. Prompt-based skill.
daily-reflection: reads memory + recent runs, reflects on patterns and suggests next steps. Prompt-based skill.
project-discovery: scans current directory structure and recent git activity, updates memory with project context. Prompt-based skill.
Each skill has: skill.yaml + prompt.md
Skills reference memory paths relative to .continuum/
prompts are clear, actionable, and reference the right files
userStories.notes: These are prompt.md files that get passed to claude -p. The prompts should instruct Claude to read memory, do the work, and write results back. Simple but effective.

userStories.title: continuum doctor and status
userStories.description: As a user, I want to verify my installation and see current status
acceptanceCriteria: `continuum doctor` checks: .continuum/ exists, config valid, memory dirs exist, skills present, claude CLI available
Reports each check as PASS/FAIL with helpful fix suggestions
Returns exit code 0 if all pass, 1 if any fail
`continuum status` shows: memory stats (file count, last modified), bootstrap status (scanned or not), skills available, last 5 runs
Both commands work without errors even on fresh install
userStories.notes: doctor is critical for debugging install issues. Keep output clean and actionable.

userStories.title: CLAUDE.md template and memory conventions
userStories.description: As a user, I want a CLAUDE.md that teaches Claude how to use the continuum system
acceptanceCriteria: .continuum/templates/CLAUDE.md is generated during init with the user's config values interpolated
CLAUDE.md sections: Identity (name, domain), Memory (where files are, how to read/write), Skills (how to invoke), Conventions (file formats, naming), Preferences (from bootstrap if available)
Instructions tell Claude to: read memory at session start, write insights to working memory, never overwrite consolidated memory, use skill prompts for recurring tasks
Template uses Jinja2-style placeholders replaced during init (or simple string.format)
A copy command or suggestion to place it in the project root or ~/.claude/
userStories.notes: This is the bridge between continuum and Claude Code. Must be clear and concise. 1-2 pages max.

userStories.title: Skill scaffolding - continuum skills new
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create my own custom skills easily
acceptanceCriteria: `continuum skills new <name>` creates .continuum/skills/local/<name>/ with skill.yaml and prompt.md templates
skill.yaml template has all fields with helpful comments
prompt.md template has structure: context section, task section, output section
Prints instructions on how to customize and run the new skill
Validates skill name (kebab-case, no conflicts with existing)
userStories.notes: Low friction skill creation is key for adoption.

userStories.title: Integration test and end-to-end flow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify the full flow works end-to-end
acceptanceCriteria: Test script or pytest that: runs init in temp dir, creates fake .jsonl transcripts, runs scan, verifies bootstrap memory created, runs doctor (all pass), runs status, lists skills, creates a new skill
All commands exit 0
No crashes on empty state
No crashes on missing claude CLI (graceful degradation)
README has 'Quick Start' section that a new user can follow in 2 minutes
userStories.notes: This is the final validation. If a friend can clone, install, init, scan and see results - we ship.
